metadata.description: Archive of monthly survey fraud delta reports for repetition detection

top_deltas.delta: Bot traffic prevalence quantified at scale — Anura reports systematic measurement of bot composition in internet traffic, with direct applicability to survey entry streams
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Likely indicates that bot-sourced survey traffic is now measurable as a distinct cohort; survey platforms may be receiving instrumented visibility into bot penetration rates. This shifts defense posture from reactive (detecting anomalies) to predictive (modeling bot baseline in entry cohorts).
top_deltas.what_to_check: Entry telemetry — ASN/IP reputation distribution + bot detection rule precision/recall vs. Anura baseline; compare PureSpectrum bot-flagged entry rate against industry benchmark if available

top_deltas.delta: Ad fraud cost quantification and niche targeting patterns — Anura published specific cost analysis and affiliate marketing niche vulnerability mapping, suggesting structured bot/fraud workflows targeting high-ROI survey verticals
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Reported pattern suggests fraudsters are targeting specific survey niches (likely high-payout verticals like financial, tech, healthcare). This indicates coordinated, economics-driven entry campaigns rather than random bot noise. Survey routers may be seeing systematic over-representation in certain panel segments.
top_deltas.what_to_check: QEP telemetry — cohort velocity by study vertical + buyer niche + entry source; flag any ASN/IP clusters showing disproportionate completion in high-payout studies; validate against buyer payout matrix

top_deltas.delta: Proxy tool distribution via malware installers — Microsoft and third-party sources report ClickFix and similar malware campaigns bundling proxy/anonymization tools; 7-Zip malware variant confirms supply-chain attack vector distributing proxy capabilities
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Possible new infrastructure pathway for survey respondents to mask real location/device state. Proxy-equipped devices entering surveys would bypass geo-IP and device fingerprint consistency checks. Malware distribution suggests organized fraud operations obtaining proxy access at scale.
top_deltas.what_to_check: Entry telemetry — geo-IP vs. device timezone mismatches + proxy detection signals (known proxy IP lists, DNS leaks); device fingerprint stability scoring; cross-reference entry IPs against Verisoul proxy/VPN reputation; validate with internal referrer/redirect forensics for malware campaign signatures

top_deltas.delta: Model distillation attack campaigns targeting large language models — Anthropic reports 16M+ blocked exchanges; signals possible use of LLM-generated synthetic responses in survey completion workflows
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Likely indicates emergence of LLM-powered respondent automation (synthetic open-end answers, consistency injection). This bypasses traditional text entropy and semantic anomaly detection by generating natural-language survey responses. May explain recent uptick in high-quality-appearing but fraudulent completes.
top_deltas.what_to_check: QEP telemetry — open-end text entropy baseline + semantic clustering (check for repeated phrase patterns across different respondents); session velocity (time-to-complete vs. text length); cross-reference entry source with known LLM inference endpoints; validate with buyer checkpoint metadata for response coherence

top_deltas.delta: Data clean rooms and quality feedback loops deployed as platform-level control — Cint announced global data quality feedback loop and data clean room capability; signals industry shift toward post-hoc respondent quality validation and cross-platform reputation sharing
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Vendor capability shift suggests industry recognition of need for network-level fraud signal sharing. This may pressure fraudsters to diversify panel sources and reduce repeat entries. Implies weakening of single-panel attack repeatability.
top_deltas.what_to_check: Panel cohort telemetry — repeat entry detection across PureSpectrum + external panel sources (if shared reputation data available); validate Verisoul identity counters for cross-panel deduplication; monitor buyer acceptance rate deltas as clean room data sharing adoption increases

controls_at_risk.reason: Proxy tool distribution via malware (ClickFix, 7-Zip variant) enables fraudsters to mask real location; devices entering surveys with malware-bundled proxy will show spoofed geo-IP and timezone mismatches that bypass naive consistency validation.

controls_at_risk.control: Open-end text entropy and semantic anomaly detection
controls_at_risk.reason: LLM-powered synthetic response generation (inferred from model distillation attack scale) can produce natural-language survey answers that evade traditional entropy baselines; fraudsters can now automate response quality without human copy-paste effort.

Industry deployment of cross-panel data clean rooms and reputation feedback loops signals that fraudsters will face increasing pressure to diversify panel sources; single-panel repeat detection becomes less effective if fraudsters distribute entries across multiple panel partnerships.

Proxy and malware-bundled anonymization tools can alter device fingerprints or mask them; devices accessing surveys through compromised hosts may exhibit fingerprint inconsistency that naive stability thresholds do not capture.

monitor_next_month: Track entry cohort ASN/IP distribution for anomalies matching known proxy/malware ISPs; compare against Verisoul reputation data for new proxy emergence
Monitor QEP open-end text clustering and entropy distribution for LLM-signature patterns (e.g., repeated phrases, unusual formality, semantic consistency across unrelated studies)
Validate cross-panel deduplication effectiveness as Cint clean room adoption scales; flag any uptick in repeat respondents across buyer partnerships
Check buyer acceptance rate deltas by study vertical; flag niche-specific entry surges matching Anura-reported high-ROI survey targets
Audit referrer/redirect chains for malware campaign indicators (known malware download sites, C2 domains, ClickFix signatures)

top_deltas.delta: LLM-generated synthetic response injection via model distillation campaigns — Anthropic blocked 16M+ exchanges targeting Claude; signals emerging capability to auto-generate plausible survey responses at scale using stolen/distilled model weights
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Synthetic responses bypass traditional open-end text entropy and semantic inconsistency detection. If distilled models are being weaponized for survey completion, entry-level text quality checks (grammar, coherence, topic relevance) will fail to distinguish synthetic from human responses. This represents a material escalation from scripted/templated answers to probabilistically-indistinguishable synthetic text.
top_deltas.what_to_check: Open-end text telemetry — measure perplexity/entropy shift in text blocks from high-velocity entry cohorts; cross-ref with known distilled model fingerprints (token distribution bias, repeated phrase patterns); validate via Verisoul semantic coherence counter and session velocity anomalies

top_deltas.delta: Proxy tool distribution normalized via supply-chain compromise — Microsoft ClickFix variant and malicious 7-Zip installer confirm proxy bundling in consumer software; signals infrastructure commoditization enabling survey bot operators to obscure IP/ASN signatures
top_deltas.why_it_matters: Proxy-enabled entry streams break IP reputation and geolocation consistency checks. If malware-distributed proxies proliferate, ASN clustering and geo-IP vs. demographics validation will face increased false-positive pressure. Operators no longer require custom C2 or commercial proxy services; they embed evasion in trojanized binaries.
top_deltas.what_to_check: Entry telemetry — ASN reputation churn within sessions (entry 1 ASN ≠ entry 2 ASN within 10min); geo-IP consistency delta; referrer chain ASN mismatches; validate via MaxMind/IP2Location anomaly rate and Verisoul IP reputation counters

top_deltas.delta: Bot traffic composition quantified at internet scale — Anura reports systematic bot/legitimate traffic ratio measurement; implies survey entry streams can be modeled as fraction of larger bot ecosystem and targeted via affiliate marketing niche strategies
top_deltas.why_it_matters: This is the first published quantification of bot prevalence by vertical and niche. Combined with Anura's affiliate marketing niche targeting analysis, it suggests fraudsters are running ROI-optimized campaigns against specific survey verticals (e.g., CPG, fintech, health). Survey routers are likely exposed to structured bot ingestion from high-ROI niches.
top_deltas.what_to_check: Entry cohort analysis — segment by referrer/vertical; measure bot rate delta by niche; check session velocity and device fingerprint churn within high-value survey verticals; validate via entry source reputation dashboard and Verisoul bot score distribution by survey category

top_deltas.delta: Industry-wide data quality feedback loop and clean room deployment — Cint announced Global Data Quality Feedback Loop and data clean room capability; signals platform-level shift toward post-hoc respondent reputation sharing and cross-platform blacklisting
top_deltas.why_it_matters: This is NOT a fraud_threat; it is a strengthening control. However, it signals that survey platforms are now sharing respondent reputation data across routers. Fraudsters will adapt by: (1) rotating identities faster, (2) targeting smaller/newer platforms not yet in feedback loop, (3) spoofing device fingerprints to evade reputation tracking. This creates pressure for PureSpectrum to harden device fingerprint validation and shorten entry-to-reputation-query latency.
top_deltas.what_to_check: Respondent reputation matching — measure cache hit rate on Cint/Dynata reputation queries; track identity rotation velocity within 7-day windows; validate via device fingerprint stability telemetry and checkpoint delta timing

controls_at_risk.control: Open-end text semantic coherence check (entropy, grammar, topic relevance)
controls_at_risk.reason: Anthropic's report of LLM-distillation attacks (16M+ blocked exchanges) signals fraudsters can now auto-generate plausible survey responses indistinguishable from human text. Traditional NLP-based quality filters will fail.

controls_at_risk.control: IP/ASN reputation blacklisting and geolocation consistency validation
controls_at_risk.reason: Malware-distributed proxies (ClickFix, 7-Zip variants) commoditize IP masking. Entries can now route through diverse ASNs and proxies embedded in trojanized software, defeating single-session IP reputation checks.

controls_at_risk.control: Session-level device fingerprint matching (UA-CH, canvas, WebGL fingerprint)
controls_at_risk.reason: Industry feedback loops increase pressure for identity rotation. Fraudsters will invest in device fingerprint spoofing to evade cross-platform reputation sharing. Static fingerprint anchors will weaken.

monitor_next_month: LLM distillation campaigns expansion — track if model weights for GPT, Gemini, or Claude appear in underground forums/breach databases; measure semantic coherence anomaly rate in high-velocity open-end text cohorts
Proxy tool variant proliferation — monitor malware feeds for new trojanized software bundling proxy/socks tools; measure ASN churn and IP rotation velocity in entry streams
Affiliate marketing niche bot targeting — validate if high-ROI survey verticals (fintech, health, CPG) show disproportionate entry velocity or bot score elevation vs. baseline
Respondent reputation loop adoption — measure Cint/Dynata reputation query success rate and cache hit velocity; track if identity rotation accelerates post-announcement
